toolRequests.intentionSummary: Build the project to see the failure

data.reasoningText:
I can see the issue. The `GenerateSampleCode` target generates a `.cs` file into the intermediate output directory, but it never adds that file to the `Compile` item group. So the file is written to disk but MSBuild/Roslyn doesn't know to compile it.

The target runs `BeforeTargets="CoreCompile;BeforeCompile"`, which means it runs before compilation, but the `Compile` items are already resolved by then (or at least, the generated file isn't included).

The fix is to add an `<ItemGroup>` inside the target that adds the generated file to the `Compile` items:


Adding the generated file to the compile items...

Let me build it first to confirm the error, then apply the fix.

arguments.description: Build the project to see the failure

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "cd /tmp/sv-8e59c9f9d99b4e328f8a1e0e0d3d91e3 && dotnet build 2>&1 | tail -20", "description": "Build the project to see the failure", "initial_wait": 30}

data.content: The file is generated but never added to the `Compile` item group, so Roslyn doesn't compile it. The fix is to include the generated file in `Compile` after writing it.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Add the generated file to compilation -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.reasoningText: Confirmed. The file is generated (`Generated code file: obj/Debug/net8.0/Generated/GeneratedInfo.cs`) but the compiler doesn't know about it. The fix is to add the generated file to the `Compile` item group inside the target.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Add the generated file to compilation -->

      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.content:
**Root cause:** The `GenerateSampleCode` target wrote `GeneratedInfo.cs` to disk but never added it to the `Compile` item group. MSBuild generated the file, but Roslyn had no idea it existed.

**Fix:** Added a `<Compile Include="...">` item inside the target (after the file is written) so the generated source is included in compilation.
